content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB of length MMM, define a function f(B)f(B)f(B) as follows:


Pick a positive integer XXX
Consider the array CCC of length MMM, where Ci=Bimod‚ÄČ‚ÄČXC_i = B_i\mod XCi‚Äč=Bi‚ÄčmodX
XXX is called **good** if CCC is a palindrome
f(B)=f(B) = f(B)= the number of **good** positive integers XXX for the array BBB
If there are infinitely many such XXX, then f(B)=‚ąí1f(B) = -1f(B)=‚ąí1
Given integers NNN and MMM. Find the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all arrays AAA of length NNN satisfying 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M1 \leq A_i \leq M1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M for all iii. As the answer may be large, output the answer modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input.

The one and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all arrays AAA of length NNN satisfying 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M1 \leq A_i \leq M1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M for all iii, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ąó1052*10^52‚ąó105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ąó1052*10^52‚ąó105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

A=[1,1]A = [1,1]A=[1,1]: Any value of XXX is valid. Thus, f(A)=‚ąí1f(A) = -1f(A)=‚ąí1.
A=[1,2]A = [1,2]A=[1,2]: Only X=1X = 1X=1 is good. Thus, f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1.
A=[1,3]A = [1,3]A=[1,3]: Only X=1X = 1X=1 and X=2X = 2X=2 are good. Thus, f(A)=2f(A) = 2f(A)=2.
A=[2,1]A = [2,1]A=[2,1]: Only X=1X = 1X=1 is good. Thus, f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1.
A=[2,2]A = [2,2]A=[2,2]: Any value of XXX is valid. Thus, f(A)=‚ąí1f(A) = -1f(A)=‚ąí1.
A=[2,3]A = [2,3]A=[2,3]: Only X=1X = 1X=1 is good. Thus, f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1.
A=[3,1]A = [3,1]A=[3,1]: Only X=1X = 1X=1 and X=2X = 2X=2 are good. Thus, f(A)=2f(A) = 2f(A)=2.
A=[3,2]A = [3,2]A=[3,2]: Only X=1X = 1X=1 is good. Thus, f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1.
A=[3,3]A = [3,3]A=[3,3]: Any value of XXX is valid. Thus, f(A)=‚ąí1f(A) = -1f(A)=‚ąí1.

Thus, the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all possible arrays A=‚ąí1+1+2+1‚ąí1+1+2+1‚ąí1=5A = -1+1+2+1-1+1+2+1-1 = 5A=‚ąí1+1+2+1‚ąí1+1+2+1‚ąí1=5.
